children.heading: Establishment of panel
children.label: a Establishment of panel
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall establish an advisory panel under this chapter to develop recommendations to expand the application of ecosystem principles in fishery conservation and management activities.October 11, 1996
individuals with expertise in the structures, functions, and physical and biological characteristics of ecosystems; and
representatives from the Councils, States, fishing industry, conservation organizations, or others with expertise in the management of marine resources.

children.heading: Recommendations
children.label: c Recommendations
children.text: Prior to selecting advisory panel members, the Secretary shall, with respect to panel members described in subsection (b)(1), solicit recommendations from the National Academy of Sciences.
an analysis of the extent to which ecosystem principles are being applied in fishery conservation and management activities, including research activities;
proposed actions by the Secretary and by the Congress that should be undertaken to expand the application of ecosystem principles in fishery conservation and management; and
such other information as may be appropriate.

children.heading: Procedural matter
children.label: e Procedural matter
children.text: The advisory panel established under this section shall be deemed an advisory panel under .section 1852(g) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: recommendations for scientific data, information and technology requirements for understanding ecosystem processes, and methods for integrating such information from a variety of federal, state, and regional sources;11So in original. Probably should be capitalized.1

children.label: B
children.text: recommendations for processes for incorporating broad stake holder participation;

children.label: C
children.text: recommendations for processes to account for effects of environmental variation on fish stocks and fisheries; and

children.label: D
children.text: a description of existing and developing council efforts to implement ecosystem approaches, including lessons learned by the councils.

children.heading: Agency technical advice and assistance, regional pilot programs
children.label: 2 Agency technical advice and assistance, regional pilot programs
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to provide necessary technical advice and assistance, including grants, to the Councils for the development and design of regional pilot programs that build upon the recommendations of the advisory panel and, when completed, the study.
